barRaise.topFinding: Read "at-risk" as a process verdict, not a code verdict: this is the thirteenth consecutive daily review with zero application-code findings and the 06-20 canonical audit reads fully aligned at v0.18.4. What flipped the verdict is the strategic lens, escalated MEDIUM to HIGH this cycle because its remedy has been carried unactioned across seven prior reviews while the cost grows: HEAD has not moved since 2026-06-14 (six days), and a frozen, clean, 98%-complete tool is still drawing a daily multi-agent lens pass that only reprints the same decisions. Daily review here is attention the project does not justify, the HIGH bar in the strategic rubric. The fix is a decision, not code: cut v1.0, settle the 864 MB .git call, normalize the YaE work queue, and drop Apothecary to weekly review so the cadence stops out-running the work.

actions.title: Cut v1.0, mark "Symbol + label polish" done, and drop Apothecary to weekly (constellation-only) review. Eighth review carrying this; first flagged HIGH.
Decide and close the 864 MB `.git` bloat: `git filter-repo --strip-blobs-bigger-than 5M` + force-push, or a "Won't fix" locked decision in PROJECT_SPEC section 8.

Normalize the queue to one project ID (`yaa`) with a `prompt` field, close the shipped `yaa-commit-release-automsg-2026-06-12`, and clear the YaE-side mirror-stale-tree item. (Orchestration side; read-only here.)
